description: Request to exempt legacy-batch-reconciler from SIEM integration requirement. Service is undergoing active decommission per approved change request CHG-2025-1847.
justification: Service decommission was approved on October 15, 2025. Final batch job ran October 28, 2025. Service shutdown scheduled for October 30, 2025. Data migration to data-pipeline service (AC-SVC-011) completed October 25, 2025. No business value in integrating a service that will be offline within 2 days.
compensating_controls: 1. Service has been in read-only mode since October 25 (data migration complete). 2. Network access restricted to management VPC only. 3. All historical logs retained in S3 archive. 4. Data-pipeline service (AC-SVC-011), which replaces this service, is fully integrated with SIEM. 5. Service will be completely shut down by October 30, 2025.
review_notes: Service was successfully decommissioned on October 30, 2025. EKS pods terminated, DNS records removed November 5. CMDB entry pending cleanup (scheduled January 2026). Exception closed — no further action required.

description: Request for temporary SLA exemption during planned infrastructure migration (July 20-22, 2025). Expected high volume of false positive alerts during migration.
justification: Planned migration of monitoring infrastructure from Splunk Cloud to Splunk Enterprise on-premises. Expected 200+ false positive alerts during migration cutover. On-call team will be overwhelmed if held to standard SLAs during this period.
compensating_controls: 1. Additional security engineers on standby during migration. 2. Pre-migration review of critical alert rules. 3. Migration conducted during lowest-traffic window. 4. Automated blocking rules remain active regardless of SIEM status.
review_notes: Migration completed successfully July 21. Exception expired as scheduled. No security incidents during migration window.

description: Request for 10-day extension on SIEM integration SLA for ml-inference-engine. Complex log format requires custom Splunk TA development.
justification: ML inference engine generates logs in a custom binary format that requires a custom Splunk Technical Add-on (TA) for parsing. Development and testing of the TA requires an additional 10 days beyond the standard 5-day SLA.
compensating_controls: 1. CloudWatch Logs active with basic alerting. 2. Service deployed behind API gateway with existing monitoring. 3. Daily manual log review during extension period.
review_notes: TA developed and deployed. SIEM integration completed April 14, within the extended timeline.
